summary: Task 077 is complete in substance. The repo now documents one env-backed `API_KEY` as the only credential for this wave, the `POST /api/open/notes` boundary fails closed with `503` when disabled and `401` for missing/invalid `X-API-Key`, the authorized path remains a `501` stub with no persistence or publish behavior, no CORS/browser-auth/multi-key surface was introduced, focused unit coverage exists for the auth boundary, and the provided deterministic evidence shows `npm run verify` passing.
satisfied_exit_criteria: `API_KEY` is documented as the sole external note-create credential in `.env.example`, `.env.compose.example`, `docs/product-specs/external-note-api.md`, `docs/design-docs/external-note-api-boundary.md`, and the active task plan.
`src/lib/external-note-api/auth.ts` fails closed when `API_KEY` is unset (`disabled`) and when `X-API-Key` is missing or invalid (`unauthorized`).
`src/app/api/open/notes/route.ts` maps the fail-closed states to `503` and `401`, and keeps the valid-key path as a `501` skeleton without note persistence.

The implementation stays narrow and server-only: one static key, one POST route, no `OPTIONS` handler, no CORS headers, no browser-session auth coupling, and no multi-key management code surfaced in the repo.

`tests/unit/external-note-api-auth.test.ts` covers the disabled auth helper case, valid header acceptance, missing/invalid header rejection, disabled route behavior, unauthorized route behavior, and the authorized stub path.
The supplied deterministic run shows `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test:unit`, and `npm run verify` all passing.
